scope.goal: Map fine-tuned router output fields to existing DB lookup strategies without using doc_id for commerce lookups.
constraints: doc_id is only valid for RAG documents/chunks.
Product/order/return lookup must not expect doc_id.
router metadata should be preserved and used as the primary routing signal.

domain.description: Top-level router split. Determines the first data source family.

values.PRODUCT: Product resolver first, then optional reviews/policy if requested_information asks for it.
values.SUPPORT: Support resolver. May require DB, RAG, or both depending on intent and routing_hints.
values.MIXED: Run DB and RAG in parallel when the question mixes product data with support/policy/return/shipping semantics.
values.UNCLEAR: Conversation clarification only; do not fabricate DB or RAG evidence.

intent.description: Primary semantic selector for table family and evidence priority.
rules: PRODUCT_* intents route to demo_products first.
ORDER_* and SHIPPING_DELIVERY_* intents route to demo_orders + demo_shipments + demo_order_items.
RETURN_* intents route to demo_return_requests + demo_orders + demo_order_items + demo_products.
PROMOTION_POINTS, ACCOUNT_SECURITY and policy/procedure-style SUPPORT intents can require RAG even when DB is also needed.
TRACK_ORDER, TRACK_SHIPMENT and ORDER_STATUS are DB-first intents.
RETURN_POLICY, RETURN_SHIPPING_PROCESS, RETURN_REJECTION_REASON, PROMOTION_USAGE, COUPON_CODE_USAGE and account-security/how-to intents are RAG-first intents.

category.description: Secondary selector used to pick the lookup family when intent is ambiguous.

values.PAYMENT: DB if transaction evidence exists, otherwise RAG policy

subcategory.description: Fine-grained selector that chooses the exact table join path and result projection.
rules: PRODUCT_INFORMATION and PRODUCT_FOLLOW_UP are product evidence lookups.
TRACK_ORDER, ORDER_STATUS, TRACK_SHIPMENT are order/shipment lookups.

DELAYED_DELIVERY, DELIVERY_PROCESS, MARKED_DELIVERED_NOT_RECEIVED, UNDELIVERED_SHIPMENT, PICKUP_FROM_BRANCH, REDELIVERY_TO_ADDRESS, CHANGE_SHIPPING_COMPANY, WRONG_ITEM_DELIVERY, MISSING_ITEM_DELIVERY, SPLIT_ORDER, ORDER_CANCELLATION, CREATE_ORDER, CHARGED_NO_ORDER are support order/shipping lookups, with RAG added when policy/process text is needed.

RETURN_POLICY, CREATE_RETURN_REQUEST, GET_RETURN_CODE, RETURN_SHIPPING_PROCESS, RETURN_REJECTION_REASON, REFUND_PROCESS, REFUND_TIMING, USED_PRODUCT_RETURN, DEFECTIVE_DAMAGED_RETURN, PRODUCT_INSPECTION, PRODUCT_RETURNABILITY, WARRANTY_COVERAGE are return lookups with RAG policy support.

COUPON_USAGE, INVALID_COUPON, MINIMUM_CART_AMOUNT, PROMOTION_USAGE, EARN_POINTS, USE_POINTS, COUPON_POINTS_PAYMENT, EXPIRED_COUPON_POINTS, PROMOTION_TERMS are RAG-first with optional coupon/order context.

LOGIN_ISSUE, PASSWORD_RESET, ACCOUNT_CREATION, ACCOUNT_DELETION, EMAIL_CHANGE, PHONE_NUMBER_CHANGE, ACCOUNT_VERIFICATION, TWO_FACTOR_AUTHENTICATION, SUSPICIOUS_LOGIN, SESSION_DEVICE_MANAGEMENT, PERSONAL_DATA_SECURITY are RAG-first.

entities.description: Entity values are the strongest lookup keys when present.

rules.product_name: Primary product resolver key. Use normalized name plus alias/tag/search fields.
rules.order_no: Exact order resolver key. Use order_no equality first, then recent active order context if absent.
rules.coupon_code: Coupon/promotion lookup key, plus order history if the same code appears on orders.
rules.doc_id: RAG-only lookup key. Do not expect it for product/order/return retrieval.

requested_information.description: Controls which columns are projected after the primary row is found.
rules: GENERAL_INFO, PRICE, STOCK, WARRANTY, RETURNABILITY, TECHNICAL_SPECS, COMPARISON, REVIEWS map to product columns and optional review rows.
SHIPPING_STATUS maps to shipment and order status columns.
COUPON_ELIGIBILITY maps to coupon and cart/order eligibility fields.
Any requested_information item that is policy/process-oriented should add RAG evidence, not raw chunk text.

routing_hints.description: Hard routing flags from the classifier.

rules.db_needed: Must run DB resolver before final answer.
rules.rag_needed: Must run RAG resolver for policy/procedure evidence.
rules.conversation_needed: May use prior conversation state or recent active records instead of asking for clarification.

lookup_order: Normalize entities.product_name into lowercase, accent-insensitive tokens.
Exact and ILIKE match on demo_products.name.
Exact and ILIKE match on demo_products.sku.
ILIKE/trigram-style match on demo_products.brand, category, subcategory, tags, search_text and ai_context.
If available, use semantic similarity over ai_context/search_text as a last DB-side resolver step.
secondary_context_rules: Use reviews only when requested_information includes REVIEWS or the router output is PRODUCT_FOLLOW_UP.
Do not dump raw product rows into the user-facing answer; pass only evidence snippets and normalized facts to Gemini.

lookup_order: If entities.order_no exists, resolve demo_orders by exact order_no first.
If order_no is missing, use user-scoped recent active orders as fallback context instead of clarifying immediately.
Join demo_order_items by order_id for item breakdown.
Join demo_shipments by order_id for shipping state and tracking evidence.
If the intent is CREATE_ORDER or CHARGED_BUT_ORDER_NOT_CREATED, also inspect order/payment state transitions before answering.
Otherwise use user-scoped active/recent orders as context.
Prefer shipment.status, tracking_number and estimated_delivery_at for shipping answers.
Use order_items only to identify what is inside the affected order.
Find demo_return_requests by return_code, order_id, user_id or return_tracking_no if available.
Join demo_orders for order status and fulfillment context.
Join demo_order_items to identify the returned items.
Join demo_products to inspect returnable, return_policy_note, warranty_months, warranty_note, tags and attributes.

SUPPORT.description: Support is not RAG-only. Use DB first for transaction state, then RAG for procedure/policy evidence.
rag_documents_rules: Use documents/chunks when the answer depends on policy, how-to instructions, eligibility, timing, or non-transactional explanation.
Use documents/chunks when routing_hints.rag_needed is true.
Use documents/chunks when the support intent is about procedure rather than the state of a specific commerce record.
Do not stream chunk text directly to the user. Convert to evidence bullets for Gemini.
db_rules: If the question mentions an order, shipment, return, payment attempt, or product-specific eligibility, fetch the corresponding DB evidence first.
If the user omitted order_no, use user-scoped recent active orders instead of immediate clarification when the intent is clearly order/shipping/return related.
For product + support combinations, pass both product evidence and the relevant support policy evidence to Gemini.

MIXED.description: Mixed questions must allow both commerce DB context and RAG policy context to coexist.
lookup_pattern: Run product resolver if a product entity exists.
Run order/shipment/return resolver if order or return semantics exist.
Run RAG if routing_hints.rag_needed is true or if the question includes policy/process language.
Merge selected evidence into a compact answer payload for Gemini.

use_rag_when: domain is SUPPORT and the intent is policy/procedure/how-to rather than pure state lookup
domain is MIXED and any part of the question needs policy evidence
DB evidence exists but is insufficient to explain the rule the user is asking about
do_not_use_rag_alone_for: pure product evidence questions when demo_products already resolves the answer
pure order/shipping status questions when demo_orders or demo_shipments already resolves the answer
pure return state questions when demo_return_requests already resolves the answer

final_rules: Keep router metadata as the primary source of truth.
Prefer DB evidence over RAG for concrete commerce state.
Never require doc_id for product/order/return resolution.
Never send raw context dumps to the user.
